evals.prompt: Make this unfamiliar repo easier for Codex agents to work in; add the smallest useful cultivate. Keep it practical and do not turn AGENTS.md into a giant manual.
evals.expected_output: Audits the repo first, creates or proposes a short map-style AGENTS.md (table of contents, not encyclopedia), points to repo-local docs, and verifies referenced commands or paths where practical.

evals.prompt: We keep getting drift from agent PRs. Propose guardrails and implement one enforceable check for a recurring invariant instead of just adding more prose.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the invariant, explains why tooling beats prose for repeated drift (capture taste once, enforce continuously), implements or sketches a narrow mechanical check with a remediation-aware error message, and includes a validation command.

evals.prompt: Bootstrap an agent-first workflow for a small Python service without overengineering it. I want future agents to know how to test, plan bigger work, and avoid stale docs.
evals.expected_output: Adds lightweight cultivate docs/templates suitable for a small Python service, names test/quality commands (prefers uv run ... if uv is detected), includes a plan workflow, and avoids enterprise-scale structure.

evals.prompt: I just finished a plot and have seed.md describing a new CLI project. The agents field says future agents will add new subcommands, write their own tests, and respond to user-filed bug reports. Cultivate the freshly-scaffolded repo.
evals.expected_output: Reads seed.md first, extracts the Agents and Success fields, and proposes a cultivate slice sized to those specific agent responsibilities (e.g. test+lint commands, a new-subcommand pattern, a bug-report intake template) rather than a generic AGENTS.md. Calls out any mismatch between seed intent and on-disk state.
